manifest.name: Random Task Selector

name: Random Task Selector

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
First public release of **Random Task Selector** — put your cursor in a Markdown checklist, run one command, and let the plugin pick your next task for you.

## Features

- **Pick for me** — draws a uniformly random task from the checklist at your cursor. Refuses to run if there are no unchecked tasks or one is already in progress.
- **In-progress marking** — the winner gets an active tag (`#in-progress` by default), a start glyph (🚀), and a selected-at timestamp. At most one active task per checklist.
- **Completion stamping** — checking off any hyphen task stamps a completed glyph (✅) and a minute-precision completed-at datetime (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm`).
- **Reactivation** — un-checking a completed task refreshes its start time and returns it to the candidate pool.
- **Configurable** — the active tag and both glyphs are editable in settings.

## Install (manual)

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below into
`<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/random-task-selector/`, then enable the plugin
under **Settings → Community plugins**.

Requires Obsidian 1.5.0 or later.
